---

## Verdict: MODERATE (7.6/10)

The Costco audit scores **7.6/10 (MODERATE)**. The core data — traffic, financials, executive quotes, ROI math, and browser findings — is solid and mostly verified. Three categories of issues require correction before sharing: (1) the `top_channels` array in costco-audit-data.json contains stale, incorrect channel share values that contradict the correct `traffic.channels` dict in the same file; (2) competitor traffic figures are from a different time period than the prospect traffic figure; (3) the Decathlon case study URL is dead and the cited "+25% search-driven revenue" metric is unverifiable. All core search gaps documented in browser testing are confirmed. All ROI math checks out. Five out of five key exec quotes verified verbatim.

---

## Warnings — Review Before Sharing

| # | Issue | Status | Details | Recommendation |
|---|-------|--------|---------|---------------|
| 1 | Walmart traffic stale (30.4% drift) | [STALE] | Audit: 648.3M, SimilarWeb Jan 2025: 451.4M | Update to Jan 2025 period (451.4M) for consistency; or note different period explicitly |
| 2 | Target traffic stale (38.3% drift) | [STALE] | Audit: 297M, SimilarWeb Jan 2025: 183.2M | Update to Jan 2025 period (183.2M) for consistency |
| 3 | Gymshark +150% metric imprecise | [UNVF] | Original Gymshark page 404; secondary sources confirm +150% mobile order rate on Black Friday specifically, not general search conversion | Update claim to "150% increase in order rate on Black Friday" or use gymshark-headless metrics |
| 4 | Walmart GenAI search platform claim | [UNVF] | Audit says "iOS, Android, and web" — Walmart blog says iOS first, Android/web "coming soon" (Jan 2024) | Minor; likely resolved by now (Jan 2026). Low risk. |

## Dimension 2: Math & Logic

### ROI Recalculation

| Component | Reported | Recalculated | Match? |
|-----------|----------|-------------|--------|
| E-commerce Revenue | $19.6B | $19.6B (10-K FY2025 direct) | Yes |
| Search Share | 15% | 15% (industry benchmark) | Yes (input) |
| Search-Addressable Revenue | $2.94B | $2.940B | Yes |
| Conservative (5%) | $147M | $147M | **Yes** |
| Moderate (10%) | $294M | $294M | **Yes** |
| Aggressive (15%) | $441M | $441M | **Yes** |

Note: 08-financial-profile.md uses $19.82B (formula-derived: $275.235B × 7.2%) giving conservative $148.6M / moderate $297.3M. JSON uses $19.6B (direct 10-K figure) giving $147M / $294M. JSON is more accurate; scratchpad is a minor rounding issue.

| Total areas | 10 | 10 | Yes |
| Sum of weights (denominator) | 13.5 | 13.5 | Yes |
| Numerator (sum score×weight) | 47.5 | 47.5 | Yes |
| Overall score | 3.5 (JSON) | 3.52 → 3.5 | **Yes (JSON correct)** |
| 10-scoring-matrix.md denominator | 13.0 | Should be 13.5 | **[FAIL] — scratchpad wrong** |

All Costco.com traffic metrics in the audit are a perfect match to the live SimilarWeb MCP re-pull (same Jan 2025 period, same ww/total parameters). This confirms the live MCP re-pull on 2026-03-19 was done correctly.

### Competitor Traffic (SimilarWeb MCP Jan 2025, ww, total vs audit figures)

| Competitor | Audit Value | SimilarWeb Jan 2025 | Drift | Status |
|-----------|------------|---------------------|-------|--------|
| Walmart | 648.3M | 451.4M | -30.4% | [STALE] — audit used different period |
| Target | 297M | 183.2M | -38.3% | [STALE] — audit used different period |
| Sam's Club | 43.3M | 46.1M | +6.5% | [PASS] |
| BJ's | 8.6M | 9.8M | +14.0% | [PASS] |

Note: Walmart and Target figures in the audit appear to come from a high-traffic period (likely Nov/Dec 2025 holiday season), which would explain the large drift. This is a methodology issue — competitor traffic should use the same period as prospect traffic.

## Dimension 7: Browser Observation Fidelity

### Screenshot Coverage

- Expected screenshots (from 09-browser-findings.md): 25
- Found on disk: 26 (includes 12b-mobile-search.png and error-state.png as extras)
- Missing from disk vs. scratchpad references: 2
  - `08-non-product-return-policy.png` (the actual redirect page, 6KB — listed in scratchpad index but not on disk. The SAYT version exists: `08-non-product-return-policy-sayt.png`)
  - `13-nlp-gaming-tv.png` (the 28KB Access Denied page — listed in scratchpad but on disk only `13-nlp-gaming-tv-sayt.png` exists)
- Coverage: 23/25 expected files = **92%** (strong)

All files on disk have non-zero sizes (smallest: error-state.png at 109KB). No corrupted files.

### Observation Cross-Reference

| Test Step | Scratchpad (09-browser-findings.md) | JSON Finding | Match? | Status |
|-----------|-------------------------------------|-------------|--------|--------|
| 2b: Empty state | "No popup...no popular/trending/recent searches" | G01: "completely blank state" | Yes | [PASS] |
| 2e: Typo "samung tv" | "11 results...No 'Did you mean Samsung?'" | G05: "11 Samsung TV results...NO correction prompt" | Yes | [PASS] |
| 2f: Synonym couch/sofa | "306 results...exact same count" | S03: "couch = 306 results. sofa = 306 results" | Yes | [PASS] |
| 2g: Gibberish "asdfghjk" | "Returns 3 results...garbage disposals!" | G02: "3 results — garbage disposals (InSinkErator, Hamilton Beach)" | Yes | [PASS] |
| 2h: "return policy" | "Redirects to customerservice.costco.com" | G03: "routes users to separate domains" | Yes | [PASS] |
| 2m: NLP query | "Access Denied — WAF blocked" | G04: "Access Denied — Akamai WAF blocks" | Yes | [PASS] |
| 2i: Kirkland | "300 results for private label brand" | S02: "Kirkland → 300 private label results" | Yes | [PASS] |
| 2r: PDP recommendations | "Could not navigate to PDP...selector issue" | G07: "PDP navigation failed during audit" | Yes | [PASS] |
| 2k: SAYT for "laptop" | "Rich SAYT...product images, specs, descriptions" | S01: "Rich product SAYT with images, specs" | Yes | [PASS] |
| Scoring: 09-browser vs JSON | Scratchpad gives 4.4/10 (10-category model) vs JSON 3.5/10 (weighted model) | JSON uses different area mapping and correct weights | NOTED | [CONS] — different scoring models |

All 10 core gap/strength findings in JSON are traceable to specific observations in 09-browser-findings.md with matching evidence.

The two [UNVF] items are screenshot-related:
- `08-non-product-return-policy.png` referenced in scratchpad index but not on disk (SAYT version exists and covers the gap adequately)
- `13-nlp-gaming-tv.png` referenced in scratchpad (28KB Access Denied page) but not on disk; `error-state.png` used as substitute in JSON
